nameMeanings.name: Payen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.

variants.name: Payan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Ramon, Carlos, Jesus, Leticia, Luis, Ana, Cruz, Juana, Roberto, Rodolfo.
Spanish (Payán): perhaps from Mozarab päiên ‘cave, ravine’, from Hispanic Latin pedia, derived from pedem ‘foot’. In Spain, this surname is found mainly in Andalusia.

variants.name: Pesnell
meanings: Probably an altered form of French (Normandy) Pesnel: from a vernacular pet form of the old personal name Payen , or a topographic name for someone who lived by a fence or palissade, from a shortened form of paissenel, pessenel, a diminutive of paisson, pesson ‘post, stake’. This surname was brought to North America from England.

variants.name: Pean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Georges, Michel, Colette, Fabienne, Herve, Jacques, Maryse, Pierre.

variants.name: Payne
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

nameMeanings.name: Puella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Louella
meanings: Modern coinage from the first syllable of Louise + the productive suffix -ella (an Italian or Latinate feminine diminutive). It is particularly associated with the Hollywood gossip columnist Louella Parsons ( 1880–1972 ).

en.lifeSummary: Puella Payen had at least 1 son with Robert de Beaumetz I. She died in Bommiers, Indre, Centre-Val de Loire, France.
